items.title: Icarus Robotics to test its free-flying robot in the ISS with Voyager
items.summary: Icarus Robotics hopes its free-flying robot will be able to do repetitive tasks for astronauts on the International Space Station. The post Icarus Robotics to test its free-flying robot in the ISS with Voyager appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Shield AI raises $2B for defense drone development, to acquire Aechelon
items.summary: Shield AI is acquiring Aechelon, which provides high-fidelity simulation for aircraft testing and pilot training. The post Shield AI raises $2B for defense drone development, to acquire Aechelon appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Mind Robotics raises Series A to develop AI-driven industrial automation
items.summary: Mind Robotics has raised millions of dollars to apply production data to AI robots with human-like dexterity, adaptability, and reasoning. The post Mind Robotics raises Series A to develop AI-driven industrial automation appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: IntBot humanoid robot greets visitors to San Jose Airport
items.summary: San José Mineta International Airport has deployed Jose, a multilingual IntBot humanoid, to provide real-time traveler assistance. The post IntBot humanoid robot greets visitors to San Jose Airport appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: VLA-OPD: Bridging Offline SFT and Online RL for Vision-Language-Action Models via On-Policy Distillation
items.summary: Although pre-trained Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models exhibit impressive generalization in robotic manipulation, post-training remains crucial to ensure reliable performance during deployment.

items.title: Ruka-v2: Tendon Driven Open-Source Dexterous Hand with Wrist and Abduction for Robot Learning
items.summary: Lack of accessible and dexterous robot hardware has been a significant bottleneck to achieving human-level dexterity in robots. Last year, we released Ruka, a fully open-sourced, tendon-driven humanoid hand with 11 degrees of freedom - 2 per finger and 3 at the thumb - buildable for under $1,300.

items.title: Partial Motion Imitation for Learning Cart Pushing with Legged Manipulators
items.summary: Loco-manipulation is a key capability for legged robots to perform practical mobile manipulation tasks, such as transporting and pushing objects, in real-world environments. However, learning robust loco-manipulation skills remains challenging due to the difficulty of maintaining stable locomotion while simultaneously performing precise manipulation behaviors.

items.title: Drive-Through 3D Vehicle Exterior Reconstruction via Dynamic-Scene SfM and Distortion-Aware Gaussian Splatting
items.summary: High-fidelity 3D reconstruction of vehicle exteriors improves buyer confidence in online automotive marketplaces, but generating these models in cluttered dealership drive-throughs presents severe technical challenges. Unlike static-scene photogrammetry, this setting features a dynamic vehicle moving against heavily cluttered, static backgrounds.

items.title: Meta-Adaptive Beam Search Planning for Transformer-Based Reinforcement Learning Control of UAVs with Overhead Manipulators under Flight Disturbances
items.summary: Drones equipped with overhead manipulators offer unique capabilities for inspection, maintenance, and contact-based interaction. However, the motion of the drone and its manipulator is tightly linked, and even small attitude changes caused by wind or control imperfections shift the end-effector away from its intended path.

items.title: How gearbox ratio selection impacts inertia matching, servo tuning, and machine performance
items.summary: Sponsored by GAM. Imagine a small child trying to push a full-size adult in a shopping cart. It’s possible to get the cart moving (barely), but it would be very hard to start or stop. Steering could be disastrous—the child may not make it around a corner in time.

items.title: AutoWeather4D: Autonomous Driving Video Weather Conversion via G-Buffer Dual-Pass Editing
items.summary: Generative video models have significantly advanced the photorealistic synthesis of adverse weather for autonomous driving; however, they consistently demand massive datasets to learn rare weather scenarios.

items.title: User Involvement in Robotic Wheelchair Development: A Decade of Limited Progress
items.summary: Robotic wheelchairs (RWs) offer significant potential to enhance autonomy and participation for people with mobility impairments, yet many systems have failed to achieve sustained real-world adoption.

items.title: The Multi-AMR Buffer Storage, Retrieval, and Reshuffling Problem: Exact and Heuristic Approaches
items.summary: Buffer zones are essential in production systems to decouple sequential processes. In dense floor storage environments, such as space-constrained brownfield facilities, manual operation is increasingly challenged by severe labor shortages and rising operational costs. Automating these zones requires solving the Buffer Storage, Retrieval, and Reshuffling Problem (BSRRP).

items.title: Addressing Ambiguity in Imitation Learning through Product of Experts based Negative Feedback
items.summary: Programming robots to perform complex tasks is often difficult and time consuming, requiring expert knowledge and skills in robot software and sometimes hardware. Imitation learning is a method for training robots to perform tasks by leveraging human expertise through demonstrations. Typically, the assumption is that those demonstrations are performed by a single, highly competent expert.

items.title: Adapt as You Say: Online Interactive Bimanual Skill Adaptation via Human Language Feedback
items.summary: Developing general-purpose robots capable of autonomously operating in human living environments requires the ability to adapt to continuously evolving task conditions. However, adapting high-dimensional coordinated bimanual skills to novel task variations at deployment remains a fundamental challenge.

items.title: DTP-Attack: A decision-based black-box adversarial attack on trajectory prediction
items.summary: Trajectory prediction systems are critical for autonomous vehicle safety, yet remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks that can cause catastrophic traffic behavior misinterpretations. Existing attack methods require white-box access with gradient information and rely on rigid physical constraints, limiting real-world applicability.

items.title: 120 Minutes and a Laptop: Minimalist Image-goal Navigation via Unsupervised Exploration and Offline RL
items.summary: The prevailing paradigm for image-goal visual navigation often assumes access to large-scale datasets, substantial pretraining, and significant computational resources. In this work, we challenge this assumption. We show that we can collect a dataset, train an in-domain policy, and deploy it to the real world (1) in less than 120 minutes, (2) on a consumer laptop, (3) without any human intervention.

items.title: Generalizable task-oriented object grasping through LLM-guided ontology and similarity-based planning
items.summary: Task-oriented grasping (TOG) is more challenging than simple object grasping because it requires precise identification of object parts and careful selection of grasping areas to ensure effective and robust manipulation.

items.title: T-800: An 800 Hz Data Glove for Precise Hand Gesture Tracking
items.summary: Human dexterity relies on rapid, sub-second motor adjustments, yet capturing these high-frequency dynamics remains an enduring challenge in biomechanics and robotics. Existing motion capture paradigms are compromised by a trade-off between temporal resolution and visual occlusion, failing to record the fine-grained hand motion of fast, contact-rich manipulation.

items.title: HandVQA: Diagnosing and Improving Fine-Grained Spatial Reasoning about Hands in Vision-Language Models
items.summary: Understanding the fine-grained articulation of human hands is critical in high-stakes settings such as robot-assisted surgery, chip manufacturing, and AR/VR-based human-AI interaction. Despite achieving near-human performance on general vision-language benchmarks, current vision-language models (VLMs) struggle with fine-grained spatial reasoning, especially in interpreting complex and articulated hand poses.

items.title: Realtime-VLA V2: Learning to Run VLAs Fast, Smooth, and Accurate
items.summary: In deployment of the VLA models to real-world robotic tasks, execution speed matters. In previous work arXiv:2510.26742 we analyze how to make neural computation of VLAs on GPU fast. However, we leave the question of how to actually deploy the VLA system on the real robots open.

items.title: VDMA: VDA 5050 V3 will help mobile robot fleets scale
items.summary: Version 3 of the VDA 5050 communications framework serves as a toolkit for mixed fleets of mobile robots, says German association VDMA. The post VDMA: VDA 5050 V3 will help mobile robot fleets scale appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Optimal Prioritized Dissipation and Closed-Form Damping Limitation under Actuator Constraints for Haptic Interfaces
items.summary: In haptics, guaranteeing stability is essential to ensure safe interaction with remote or virtual environments. One of the most relevant methods at the state-of-the-art is the Time Domain Passivity Approach (TDPA). However, its high conservatism leads to a significant degradation of transparency. Moreover, the stabilizing action may conflict with the device's physical limitations.

items.title: Curvature-aware Expected Free Energy as an Acquisition Function for Bayesian Optimization
items.summary: We propose an Expected Free Energy-based acquisition function for Bayesian optimization to solve the joint learning and optimization problem, i.e., optimize and learn the underlying function simultaneously. We show that, under specific assumptions, Expected Free Energy reduces to Upper Confidence Bound, Lower Confidence Bound, and Expected Information Gain.

items.title: DiffusionAnything: End-to-End In-context Diffusion Learning for Unified Navigation and Pre-Grasp Motion
items.summary: Efficiently predicting motion plans directly from vision remains a fundamental challenge in robotics, where planning typically requires explicit goal specification and task-specific design. Recent vision-language-action (VLA) models infer actions directly from visual input but demand massive computational resources, extensive training data, and fail zero-shot in novel scenes.

items.title: DFM-VLA: Iterative Action Refinement for Robot Manipulation via Discrete Flow Matching
items.summary: Vision--Language--Action (VLA) models that encode actions using a discrete tokenization scheme are increasingly adopted for robotic manipulation, but existing decoding paradigms remain fundamentally limited.

items.title: Line-of-Sight-Constrained Multi-Robot Mapless Navigation via Polygonal Visible Regions
items.summary: Multi-robot systems rely on underlying connectivity to ensure reliable communication and timely coordination. This paper studies the line-of-sight (LoS) connectivity maintenance problem in multi-robot navigation with unknown obstacles. Prior works typically assume known environment maps to formulate LoS constraints between robots, which hinders their practical deployment.

items.title: DRUM: Diffusion-based Raydrop-aware Unpaired Mapping for Sim2Real LiDAR Segmentation
items.summary: LiDAR-based semantic segmentation is a key component for autonomous mobile robots, yet large-scale annotation of LiDAR point clouds is prohibitively expensive and time-consuming. Although simulators can provide labeled synthetic data, models trained on synthetic data often underperform on real-world data due to a data-level domain gap. To address this issue, we propose DRUM, a novel Sim2Real translation framework.

items.title: SwarmCoDe: A Scalable Co-Design Framework for Heterogeneous Robot Swarms via Dynamic Speciation
items.summary: Robot swarms offer inherent robustness and the capacity to execute complex, collaborative tasks surpassing the capabilities of single-agent systems. Co-designing these systems is critical, as marginal improvements in individual performance or unit cost compound significantly at scale. However, under traditional frameworks, this scale renders co-design intractable due to exponentially large, non-intuitive design spaces.

items.title: 4DRaL: Bridging 4D Radar with LiDAR for Place Recognition using Knowledge Distillation
items.summary: Place recognition is crucial for loop closure detection and global localization in robotics. Although mainstream algorithms typically rely on cameras and LiDAR, these sensors are susceptible to adverse weather conditions. Fortunately, the recently developed 4D millimeter-wave radar (4D radar) offers a promising solution for all-weather place recognition.

items.title: Agile Robots to deploy Google DeepMind foundation models on its humanoid
items.summary: Agile Robots said the partnership will focus on high-value industrial use cases in sectors with acute and growing demand for automation. The post Agile Robots to deploy Google DeepMind foundation models on its humanoid appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Fast-dVLA: Accelerating Discrete Diffusion VLA to Real-Time Performance
items.summary: This paper proposes a novel approach to address the challenge that pretrained VLA models often fail to effectively improve performance and reduce adaptation costs during standard supervised finetuning (SFT). Some advanced finetuning methods with auxiliary training objectives can improve performance and reduce the number of convergence steps.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
items.summary: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a turning point in physical AI: Robots, vehicles and factories are scaling from single use cases and […]

items.title: MMaDA-VLA: Large Diffusion Vision-Language-Action Model with Unified Multi-Modal Instruction and Generation
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models aim to control robots for manipulation from visual observations and natural-language instructions. However, existing hierarchical and autoregressive paradigms often introduce architectural overhead, suffer from temporal inconsistency and long-horizon error accumulation, and lack a mechanism to capture environment dynamics without extra modules.

items.title: Zoox sets new geographic milestones, product features for its robotaxi
items.summary: Amazon-owned Zoox is expanding its purpose-built robotaxi service to San Francisco, Las Vegas, Austin, and Miami, marking a national rollout. The post Zoox sets new geographic milestones, product features for its robotaxi appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Why connectivity is the bottleneck for BVLOS autonomous systems
items.summary: Satellite connectivity enables autonomous drones to operate in remote areas, overcoming the limitations of terrestrial networks for critical BVLOS missions. The post Why connectivity is the bottleneck for BVLOS autonomous systems appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: 3 robotics trends from NVIDIA GTC 2026
items.summary: Our reporter shares major AI and robotics takeaways from speakers and exhibitors at NVIDIA's GTC event in San Jose last week. The post 3 robotics trends from NVIDIA GTC 2026 appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Unitree IPO shows a real hardware business, but the humanoid case is still early
items.summary: While Unitree IPO is growing as a credible hardware business, industrial humanoid robots have a ways to go, says one roboticist. The post Unitree IPO shows a real hardware business, but the humanoid case is still early appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Basler and Orbbec partner for 3D vision systems for mobile robots
items.summary: Orbbec and Basler unveiled at LogiMAT the Stereo mini, which combines 3D cameras and computer vision for logistics and other applications. The post Basler and Orbbec partner for 3D vision systems for mobile robots appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Lucid Bots raises Series B funding as operator network hits $75M in revenue
items.summary: Lucid Bots has raised a total of $34 million, as its drones collect data helping its owner-operators clean more consistently. The post Lucid Bots raises Series B funding as operator network hits $75M in revenue appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: NORD Drivesystems to show modular systems for intelligent automation at MODEX
items.summary: NORD says its modular product portfolio offers high configurability for compact, efficient, and reliable drive systems engineered to individual application needs. The post NORD Drivesystems to show modular systems for intelligent automation at MODEX appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Amazon acquires humanoid developer Fauna Robotics
items.summary: Amazon said it's taking a 'well thought-out and measured approach' to understanding how personal robots could improve their customers' lives. The post Amazon acquires humanoid developer Fauna Robotics appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: ABot-PhysWorld: Interactive World Foundation Model for Robotic Manipulation with Physics Alignment
items.summary: Video-based world models offer a powerful paradigm for embodied simulation and planning, yet state-of-the-art models often generate physically implausible manipulations - such as object penetration and anti-gravity motion - due to training on generic visual data and likelihood-based objectives that ignore physical laws.

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Live Updates on What’s Next in AI
items.summary: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

items.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
items.summary: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.

items.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest US Navy robotics deal yet
items.summary: Gecko Robotics inked a five-year deal to help the U.S. Navy monitor and predict needed maintenance on its fleet of ships.

items.title: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
items.summary: The Cat 306 CR mini-excavator weighs just under eight tons and fits inside a standard shipping container. It’s the machine a contractor rents when the job site is tight: a utility trench near a foundation, a basement dig in a dense neighborhood. The cab is roughly the size of a phone booth. The operator sits […]

items.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
items.summary: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

items.title: ABB Robotics Taps NVIDIA Omniverse to Deliver Industrial‑Grade Physical AI at Scale
items.summary: ABB Robotics and NVIDIA today announced a breakthrough partnership that brings industrial‑grade physical AI to the factory floor. By integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries directly into its RobotStudio programming and simulation suite, ABB Robotics will now deliver physically accurate simulation capabilities in its platform, dramatically cutting engineering time, reducing deployment costs by up to 40% and […]

items.title: NVIDIA and Global Industrial Software Leaders Partner With India’s Largest Manufacturers to Drive AI Boom
items.summary: India is entering a new age of industrialization, as AI transforms how the world designs, builds and runs physical products and systems. The country is investing $134 billion dollars in new manufacturing capacity across construction, automotive, renewable energy and robotics, creating both a massive challenge and opportunity to build software-defined factories from day one. At […]

items.title: Upside Robotics is reducing fertilizer use and waste in corn crops
items.summary: Upside Robotics builds autonomous solar-powered robots that can help farmers reduce their fertilizer use by 70%.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Physical AI Open Models and Frameworks Advance Robots and Autonomous Systems
items.summary: Open source has become essential for driving innovation in robotics and autonomy. By providing access to critical infrastructure — from simulation frameworks to AI models — NVIDIA is enabling collaborative development that accelerates the path to safer, more capable autonomous systems.

items.title: How YC-backed Bucket Robotics survived its first CES
items.summary: Now, the startup is turning its attention to building the business, fundraising, and striking commercial deals.

items.title: Oshen built the first ocean robot to collect data in a Category 5 hurricane
items.summary: Oshen has signed contracts with multiple government agencies for its C-Star robots to collect ocean data autonomously.

items.title: AI’s Next Revolution: Multiply Labs Is Scaling Robotics-Driven Cell Therapy Biomanufacturing Labs
items.summary: Startup works with leading cell therapy companies to bring robotics manufacturing into the clean room, reducing costs by more than 70% while accelerating output compared with legacy systems.

items.title: Inside CES 2026’s “physical AI” takeover
items.summary: After years of chatbots and image generators, AI is finally leaving the screen. At CES 2026, that shift became impossible to ignore.  The annual tech showcase in Las Vegas was dominated by “physical AI” and robotics, from Boston Dynamic’s newly redesigned Atlas humanoid robot to AI-powered ice makers (yes, really). The companies in attendance clearly want consumers […]

items.title: Japan Science and Technology Agency Develops NVIDIA-Powered Moonshot Robot for Elderly Care
items.summary: The next universal technology since the smartphone is on the horizon — and it may be a little less pocket friendly. The Moonshot research program, funded by the Japan Science and Technology Agency and accelerated by NVIDIA AI and robotics technologies, is working to create a world by 2050 where AI-powered, autonomously learning robots are […]

items.title: Steel, Sensors and Silicon: How Caterpillar Is Bringing Edge AI to the Jobsite
items.summary: At CES, Caterpillar reveals how it’s integrating NVIDIA technologies, from NVIDIA Jetson Thor to speech models, to transform the world’s heavy industries.

items.title: From Warehouse to Wallet: New State of AI in Retail and CPG Survey Uncovers How AI Is Rewiring Supply Chains and Customer Experiences
items.summary: AI has transformed retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) operations, enhancing customer analysis and segmentation to enable greater personalization for marketing and advertising, and boosting the speed and accuracy of demand forecasting for supply chains and logistics. Companies are also raising the bar for customer engagement through intelligent digital shopping assistants and catalog enrichment by […]

items.title: NVIDIA Unveils New Open Models, Data and Tools to Advance AI Across Every Industry
items.summary: Expanding the open model universe, NVIDIA today released new open models, data and tools to advance AI across every industry. These models — spanning the NVIDIA Nemotron family for agentic AI, the NVIDIA Cosmos platform for physical AI, the new NVIDIA Alpamayo family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA Clara […]

items.title: Pickle Robot adds Tesla veteran as first CFO
items.summary: Jeff Evanson recently joined Pickle Robot as the company's first CFO as the startup has allegedly expanded its partnership with UPS.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: OpenUSD and NVIDIA Halos Accelerate Safety for Robotaxis, Physical AI Systems
items.summary: Physical AI is moving from research labs into the real world, powering intelligent robots and autonomous vehicles (AVs) — such as robotaxis — that must reliably sense, reason and act amid unpredictable conditions.

items.title: Cheers to AI: ADAM Robot Bartender Makes Drinks at Vegas Golden Knights Game
items.summary: In Las Vegas’s T-Mobile Arena, fans of the Golden Knights are getting more than just hockey — they’re getting a taste of the future. ADAM, a robot developed with NVIDIA Isaac libraries, is pouring drinks and turning heads in one of the NHL’s most exciting venues. ADAM, short for Automated Dual Arm Mixologist, was developed […]

items.title: 1X struck a deal to send its ‘home’ humanoids to factories and warehouses
items.summary: Despite launching as a humanoid robot designed to help consumers around the house, 1X's Neo robots are heading to industrial use cases.

items.title: Toward Efficient and Robust Behavior Models for Multi-Agent Driving Simulation
items.summary: Scalable multi-agent driving simulation requires behavior models that are both realistic and computationally efficient. We address this by optimizing the behavior model that controls individual traffic participants. To improve efficiency, we adopt an instance-centric scene representation, where each traffic participant and map element is modeled in its own local coordinate frame.

items.title: Robots’ Holiday Wishes Come True: NVIDIA Jetson Platform Offers High-Performance Edge AI at Festive Prices
items.summary: Editor’s note: This blog has been updated to showcase additional recent innovations tapping into the NVIDIA Jetson platform. Developers, researchers, hobbyists and students can take a byte out of holiday shopping this season as NVIDIA has unwrapped special discounts on the NVIDIA Jetson family of developer kits for edge AI and robotics — available through […]

items.title: NVIDIA and AWS Expand Full-Stack Partnership, Providing the Secure, High-Performance Compute Platform Vital for Future Innovation
items.summary: At AWS re:Invent, NVIDIA and Amazon Web Services expanded their strategic collaboration with new technology integrations across interconnect technology, cloud infrastructure, open models and physical AI. As part of this expansion, AWS will support NVIDIA NVLink Fusion — a platform for custom AI infrastructure — for deploying its custom-designed silicon, including next-generation Trainium4 chips for […]

items.title: At NeurIPS, NVIDIA Advances Open Model Development for Digital and Physical AI
items.summary: Researchers worldwide rely on open-source technologies as the foundation of their work. To equip the community with the latest advancements in digital and physical AI, NVIDIA is further expanding its collection of open AI models, datasets and tools — with potential applications in virtually every research field. At NeurIPS, one of the world’s top AI […]

items.title: Wanderland: Geometrically Grounded Simulation for Open-World Embodied AI
items.summary: Reproducible closed-loop evaluation remains a major bottleneck in Embodied AI such as visual navigation. A promising path forward is high-fidelity simulation that combines photorealistic sensor rendering with geometrically grounded interaction in complex, open-world urban environments.

items.title: This Khosla-backed startup can track drones, trucks, and robotaxis, inch by inch
items.summary: Point One Navigation, now valued at $230 million, is building out well beyond automotive.

items.title: Powering AI Superfactories, NVIDIA and Microsoft Integrate Latest Technologies for Inference, Cybersecurity, Physical AI
items.summary: Timed with the Microsoft Ignite conference running this week, NVIDIA is expanding its collaboration with Microsoft, including through the adoption of next-generation NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet switches for the new Microsoft Fairwater AI superfactory, powered by the NVIDIA Blackwell platform. The collaboration brings new integrations across Microsoft 365 Copilot, as well as the public preview of […]

items.title: Why a researcher is building robots that look and act like bats
items.summary: These palm-sized robots use ultrasound signals to navigate harsh conditions in search and rescue missions.

items.title: AI researchers ’embodied’ an LLM into a robot – and it started channeling Robin Williams
items.summary: AI researchers at Andon Labs embedded various LLMs in a vacuum robot to test how ready they were to be embodied. And hilarity ensued.

items.title: Towards Automated Chicken Deboning via Learning-based Dynamically-Adaptive 6-DoF Multi-Material Cutting
items.summary: Automating chicken shoulder deboning requires precise 6-DoF cutting through a partially occluded, deformable, multi-material joint, since contact with the bones presents serious health and safety risks.

items.title: Coco Robotics taps UCLA professor to lead new physical AI research lab
items.summary: Coco Robotics is working toward automating its fleet of delivery robots using its millions of miles of collected data.

items.title: The world is just not quite ready for humanoids yet
items.summary: Despite the amount of money being injected into the industry, humanoids won't be able to learn dexterity -- or the fine motor movements with hands -- rendering them essentially useless.

items.title: SoftBank bulks up its robotics portfolio with ABB Group’s robotics unit
items.summary: SoftBank says this acquisition will help it dive deeper into robotics as it considers physical AI to be the next frontier.

items.title: Former OpenAI and DeepMind researchers raise whopping $300M seed to automate science
items.summary: Periodic Labs has raised from a tech industry who's who, including Andreessen Horowitz, Nvidia, Elad Gil, Jeff Dean, Eric Schmidt, and Jeff Bezos.

items.title: Live demo fails, AI safety wins, and the golden age of robotics
items.summary: This week on Equity, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, and Max Zeff unpack the biggest moves in AI, robotics, and regulation. Listen to the full episode to hear about: Equity will be back next week. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts! Equity is TechCrunch’s flagship podcast, produced by Theresa Loconsolo, and posts every Wednesday and Friday. […]

items.title: Why Runway is eyeing the robotics industry for future revenue growth
items.summary: Runway is building up a robotics-focused team and fine-tuning its existing models for robotics and self-driving car customers.

items.title: Tesla is reportedly behind on its pledge to build 5,000 Optimus bots this year
items.summary: Tesla is behind on its goal to produce at least 5,000 Optimus humanoid robots by the end of 2025.

items.title: How TRIC Robotics is reducing pesticide use on strawberries using UV light
items.summary: TRIC Robotics just raised a $5.5 million seed round for its tractor-sized robots that help eliminate bacteria and pests on strawberry plants.

items.title: Robust Route Planning for Sidewalk Delivery Robots
items.summary: Sidewalk delivery robots are a promising solution for last-mile freight distribution. Yet, they operate in dynamic environments characterized by pedestrian flows and potential obstacles, which make travel times highly uncertain and can significantly affect their efficiency.

items.title: Diligent Robotics hires two notable Cruise alumni to its leadership team
items.summary: Diligent Robotics hired Cruise's former chief operating officer and former head of AI and robotics into c-suite positions.

items.title: Gaussian Mapping for Evolving Scenes
items.summary: Mapping systems with novel view synthesis (NVS) capabilities, most notably 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), are widely used in computer vision, as well as in various applications, including augmented reality, robotics, and autonomous driving. However, many current approaches are limited to static scenes.

items.title: INSIGHT: Enhancing Autonomous Driving Safety through Vision-Language Models on Context-Aware Hazard Detection and Edge Case Evaluation
items.summary: Autonomous driving systems face significant challenges in handling unpredictable edge-case scenarios, such as adversarial pedestrian movements, dangerous vehicle maneuvers, and sudden environmental changes. Current end-to-end driving models struggle with generalization to these rare events due to limitations in traditional detection and prediction approaches.

items.title: BayesFLo: Bayesian fault localization of complex software systems
items.summary: Software testing is essential for the reliable development of complex software systems. A key step in software testing is fault localization, which uses test data to pinpoint failure-inducing combinations for further diagnosis.

items.title: CACTO-SL: Using Sobolev Learning to improve Continuous Actor-Critic with Trajectory Optimization
items.summary: Trajectory Optimization (TO) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) are powerful and complementary tools to solve optimal control problems. On the one hand, TO can efficiently compute locally-optimal solutions, but it tends to get stuck in local minima if the problem is not convex. On the other hand, RL is typically less sensitive to non-convexity, but it requires a much higher computational effort.
